evidence.quote_or_summary: There are two distinct seasons for the Miyar Valley trek – June-July (Summer) and August-September (Monsoon).

values: Summer (June-July) is dry and crisp with snow patches on the trail and hard snow closer to Miyar Glacier
Monsoon (August-September) is moist with no snow on the trek except at Miyar Glacier

evidence.quote_or_summary: While Summer is dry and crisp, Monsoon is moist.
You may find snow patches on the trail, with an increased chance of hard snow closer to Miyar Glacier.
You will not find snow, except for the Miyar Glacier in Monsoon.

values: Snow patches on trail in Summer (June-July) with hard snow closer to Miyar Glacier
No snow on trek in Monsoon except at Miyar Glacier

evidence.quote_or_summary: You may find snow patches on the trail, with an increased chance of hard snow closer to Miyar Glacier.
You will not find snow, except for the Miyar Glacier in Monsoon.

values: Monsoon season (August-September) brings moist conditions and rain/drizzle that lowers temperatures
Summer rain can drop daytime temperatures to around 15 °C

evidence.quote_or_summary: any rain or slight drizzle brings down the temperature by a few degrees.
During the day if the sun goes behind the clouds and there is about of rain, then the temperature can fall to around 15 °C.

values: Monsoon (August-September) daytime: 15-18 °C; nighttime: 0-5 °C, dipping to 0 °C in September
3 warm layers required in both seasons

evidence.quote_or_summary: Day time: Between 18 °C and 20 °C | Night time: Will drop to temperatures between 10 °C and 5 °C.
Day time: Between 15 °C and 18 °C | Night time: Will drop to temperatures between 5 °C and 0 °C.
In September the temperature dips to 0° C.
No. of warm layers required: 3 warm layers

values: Snow patches on ground above 10,000 feet in Summer after winter has left the slopes

evidence.quote_or_summary: It's that time of the year when winter has left the slopes. But, you will still find snow patches on the ground above 10,000 feet.

values: Night temperatures dip to near zero in both seasons
Rain or drizzle in Monsoon lowers temperatures further
Keep a jacket handy during Monsoon

evidence.quote_or_summary: But at night it dips to almost zero levels.
any rain or slight drizzle brings down the temperature by a few degrees. So keep a jacket handy with you always.
